title: luhtech-schema refs rebuild generator + CI drift check (D-0)
description: Built the luhtech-schema refs rebuild subcommand and validated by populating missing schema ref data (rule.schema.json, merged at a5a30ce 2026-05-31). Pure regeneration from disk truth with three documented compatibility bends: URN preservation, description preservation for canonical entries, .refsignore exclusion. CI drift check ensures refs stay in sync going forward.

outputs.description: refs.py generator module — pure regeneration from disk truth with .refsignore, URN preservation, description preservation, file path normalization, group metadata sidecars, local date for changelog.
acceptanceCriteria: luhtech-schema refs rebuild and --check both functional
Idempotent (second rebuild produces no further changes)

outputs.description: cli.py refs subparser wired at parity with existing subcommands.
acceptanceCriteria: luhtech-schema refs rebuild callable from CLI
luhtech-schema refs rebuild --check callable from CLI

outputs.description: .refsignore exclusion list with crm/** initial entry.
schemas/patent/_index.json sidecar carrying canonical patent group metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Generator reads sidecar for group entry metadata

outputs.description: schema-refs.json regenerated with 34 additions (rule + 33 previously-unregistered canonical schemas).
acceptanceCriteria: 33 previously-unregistered schemas registered (14 ROOT-LEVEL, 8 PORTFOLIO, 7 PM, 4 SERVICE-GRAPH)
Zero changes to previously-registered entries (URN ids and curated descriptions preserved)
Workflow exists and validates as YAML
Runs refs rebuild --check on PR; fails on drift
Passed on first run after merge

preMerge: refs rebuild --check exits 1 with expected drift (34 additions, no regressions to previously-registered entries)
Regression check (jq -cS sort-normalized) confirmed zero value changes to previously-registered entries
Idempotency confirmed: second rebuild produces no changes
postMerge: main shows schema-refs.json with rule.schema.json registered
refs rebuild --check exit 0 on main (clean state)
refs-check CI passed on first run

transitions.trigger: Dispatch authored after substrate research (R-1 through R-10 in foundation report 2026-05-31-rule-pipeline-foundation-report.md)
MERGE PR 54 — squash merge, branch deleted
Post-merge verification clean: rule registered, refs rebuild --check exit 0 on main, refs-check CI passed on first run

notes: First deliverable of the rule-pipeline foundation arc. Established mechanical refs registration for every subsequent schema landing. Three documented contract bends (URN preservation, description preservation, .refsignore exclusion) all principled and traceable to schema-refs v1.9.0 changelog or .refsignore content. Scope expanded mid-deliverable per memory #20 substrate-aligned enterprise-complete: from rule + bug fixes to comprehensive registry (34 additions) when scope categorization revealed hand-curated registry tracked only ~13% of disk reality.
